    78	 succeeded in 0ms:
    79	---
    80	name: codex-review
    81	version: 2.1.4
    82	author: BenedictKing
    83	description: "Professional code review skill for Claude Code. Automatically collects file changes and task status. Triggers when working directory has uncommitted changes, or reviews latest commit when clean. Triggers: code review, review, 代码审核, 代码审查, 检查代码"
    84	allowed-tools:
    85	  - Task
    86	  - Bash
    87	  - Read
    88	  - Glob
    89	  - Write
    90	  - Edit
    91	user-invocable: true
    92	---
    93	
    94	# Codex Code Review Skill
    95	
    96	## Trigger Conditions
    97	
    98	Triggered when user input contains:
    99	
   100	- "代码审核", "代码审查", "审查代码", "审核代码"
   101	- "review", "code review", "review code", "codex 审核"
   102	- "帮我审核", "检查代码", "审一下", "看看代码"
   103	
   104	## Core Concept: Intention vs Implementation
   105	
   106	Running `codex review --uncommitted` alone only shows AI "what was done (Implementation)".
   107	Recording intention first tells AI "what you wanted to do (Intention)".
   108	
   109	**"Code changes + intention description" as combined input is the most effective way to improve AI code review quality.**
   110	
   111	## Skill Architecture
   112	
   113	This skill operates in two phases:
   114	
   115	1. **Preparation Phase** (current context): Check working directory, update CHANGELOG
   116	2. **Review Phase** (isolated context): Invoke Task tool to execute Lint + codex review (using context: fork to reduce context waste)
   117	
   118	## Execution Steps
   119	
   120	### 0. [First] Check Working Directory Status
   121	
   122	```bash
   123	git diff --name-only && git status --short
   124	```
   125	
   126	**Decide review mode based on output:**
   127	
   128	- **Has uncommitted changes** → Continue with steps 1-4 (normal flow)
   129	- **Clean working directory** → Directly invoke codex-runner: `codex review --commit HEAD`
   130	
   131	### 1. [Mandatory] Check if CHANGELOG is Updated
   132	
   133	**Before any review, must check if CHANGELOG.md contains description of current changes.**
   134	
   135	```bash
   136	# Check if CHANGELOG.md is in uncommitted changes
   137	git diff --name-only | grep -E "(CHANGELOG|changelog)"
   138	```
   139	
   140	**If CHANGELOG is not updated, you must automatically perform the following (don't ask user to do it manually):**
   141	
   142	1. **Analyze changes**: Run `git diff --stat` and `git diff` to get complete changes
   143	2. **Auto-generate CHANGELOG entry**: Generate compliant entry based on code changes
   144	3. **Write to CHANGELOG.md**: Use Edit tool to insert entry at top of `[Unreleased]` section
   145	4. **Continue review flow**: Immediately proceed to next steps after CHANGELOG update
   146	
   147	**Auto-generated CHANGELOG entry format:**
   148	
   149	```markdown
   150	## [Unreleased]
   151	
   152	### Added / Changed / Fixed
   153	
   154	- Feature description: what problem was solved or what functionality was implemented
   155	- Affected files: main modified files/modules
   156	```
   157	
   158	**Example - Auto-generation Flow:**
   159	
   160	```
   161	1. Detected CHANGELOG not updated
   162	2. Run git diff --stat, found handlers/responses.go modified (+88 lines)
   163	3. Run git diff to analyze details: added CompactHandler function
   164	4. Auto-generate entry:
   165	   ### Added
   166	   - Added `/v1/responses/compact` endpoint for conversation context compression
   167	   - Supports multi-channel failover and request body size limits
   168	5. Use Edit tool to write to CHANGELOG.md
   169	6. Continue with lint and codex review
   170	```
   171	
   172	### 2. [Critical] Stage All New Files
   173	
   174	**Before invoking codex review, must add all new files (untracked files) to git staging area, otherwise codex will report P1 error.**
   175	
   176	```bash
   177	# Check for new files
   178	git status --short | grep "^??"
   179	```
   180	
   181	**If there are new files, automatically execute:**
   182	
   183	```bash
   184	# Safely stage all new files (handles empty list and special filenames)
   185	git ls-files --others --exclude-standard -z | while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do git add -- "$f"; done
   186	```
   187	
   188	**Explanation:**
   189	
   190	- `-z` uses null character to separate filenames, correctly handles filenames with spaces/newlines
   191	- `while IFS= read -r -d ''` reads filenames one by one
   192	- `git add -- "$f"` uses `--` separator, correctly handles filenames starting with `-`
   193	- When no new files exist, loop body doesn't execute, safely skipped
   194	- This won't stage modified files, only handles new files
   195	- codex needs files to be tracked by git for proper review
   196	
   197	### 3. Evaluate Task Difficulty and Invoke codex-runner
   198	
   199	**Count change scale:**
   200	
   201	```bash
   202	# Count number of changed files and lines of code
   203	git diff --stat | tail -1
   204	```
   205	
   206	**Difficulty Assessment Criteria:**
   207	
   208	**Difficult Tasks** (meets any condition):
   209	
   210	- Modified files ≥ 10
   211	- Total code changes (insertions + deletions) ≥ 500 lines
   212	- Single metric: insertions ≥ 300 lines OR deletions ≥ 300 lines
   213	- Involves core architecture/algorithm changes
   214	- Cross-module refactoring
   215	- Config: `model_reasoning_effort=xhigh`, timeout 30 minutes
   216	
   217	**Normal Tasks** (other cases):
   218	
   219	- Config: `model_reasoning_effort=high`, timeout 10 minutes
   220

**A. Source Pin Acknowledgement**

- Upstream package: `@turnkey/api-key-stamper`
- Version: `0.5.0`
- Tarball sha256: `962a2d22c7c40240f05be98533769b37ab7dad7dbb5abec762c41007233d02bd`
- C# file git SHA: `0704d7051e7a5d4a140e17e246289411292e921d`

**B. Method Coverage Table**

| C# method (file:line) | Upstream TS function (path:line) | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ApiKeyStamper(string apiPublicKey, string apiPrivateKey)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:62`) | `ApiKeyStamper.constructor(config)` (`ts-source/index.ts:78`) | REVIEWED | Stores key material, but C# also validates private key at construction; see E. |
| `Stamp(string payload)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:118`) | `ApiKeyStamper.stamp(payload)` (`ts-source/index.ts:84`) | REVIEWED | JSON stamp field names/order and `X-Stamp` value construction reviewed. |
| `SignWithApiKey(string content)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:148`) | `purejs.signWithApiKey(input)` (`ts-source/purejs.ts:5`) | REVIEWED | Mirrors purejs path: derive compressed pubkey, compare, SHA-256, P-256 ECDSA, low-S, DER hex. |
| `EncodeDerSignatureHex(BigInteger r, BigInteger s)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:199`) | `signature.toDERHex()` (`ts-source/purejs.ts:23`) | REVIEWED | Upstream DER implementation is in `@noble/curves`, not local TS; local DER positive INTEGER encoding reviewed against expected DER form. |

**C. Intentional Adaptations**

- TS config object constructor -> C# positional constructor: language/API-shape adaptation; same key values are stored.
- TS `async`/dynamic import -> synchronous C# calls: no JS module runtime in C#; output is immediate equivalent for the reviewed purejs path.
- TS object literal stamp -> C# DTO with `JsonPropertyName`: preserves `publicKey`, `scheme`, `signature` names and declaration order.
- `JSON.stringify` -> source-generated `System.Text.Json`: structural AOT adaptation; valid stamp fields are ASCII hex/constants, so emitted JSON bytes match expected field names/order.
- `@turnkey/encoding` helpers -> local `Encoding` helpers: structural port of hex/base64url helpers used for the stamp header.
- `@noble/curves` P-256 -> BouncyCastle P-256 primitives: implementation substitution for scalar multiplication and ECDSA while preserving P-256/SHA-256/DER output intent.
- Noble `toDERHex()` -> local DER writer: structural local helper for the same ASN.1 SEQUENCE of positive INTEGER `r` and `s`.

**D. D17 Enforcement Check**

N/A (not Crypto.cs).

**E. Logic Divergence Findings**

| C# file:line | TS upstream path:line | Semantic diff | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:148` and comment scope at `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:13` | `ts-source/index.ts:31`, `ts-source/index.ts:56`, `ts-source/index.ts:84` | C# always follows the purejs-style deterministic RFC6979 path. Upstream `ApiKeyStamper.stamp()` defaults through `detectRuntime()`, so in Node it dispatches to `nodecrypto.ts`, whose ECDSA signature bytes are not expected to be deterministic. Repeated C# stamps can therefore differ from upstream default Node stamps even for the same key/payload. | Make the parity target explicit in tests and docs as `runtimeOverride = "purejs"` parity, or change the C# implementation/fixtures to match the selected upstream default behavior. |
| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:62` | `ts-source/index.ts:78`, `ts-source/purejs.ts:10` | C# validates/parses the private key in the constructor and throws before any signing call. Upstream constructor only assigns fields; invalid key material is rejected later when signing derives/imports the key. This changes error timing and observable construction behavior. | Defer private-key parsing/range validation until `SignWithApiKey`, or explicitly document constructor-time validation as an intentional behavior change. |

**F. Fixture Comparison Gate**

FAIL.

- No fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` currently exercises `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md` lists `api-key-stamper/`, but that directory is absent.
- `tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs` uses upstream fixture constants inline, but it does not assert exact Node-generated stamp bytes. It verifies JSON key order, constants, DER shape, signature validity, determinism, and low-S behavior.
- Because there is no `tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper` artifact generated from the pinned upstream package, I cannot confirm “all fixtures match” for this file.

**G. Unresolved Assumptions**

- I could not run `dotnet test` in this read-only sandbox; MSBuild failed with a named-pipe `Permission denied` error.
- Exact C# signature bytes were not compared against a pinned upstream purejs-generated fixture because no such fixture exists in `tests/Fixtures/`.
- The review assumes the intended parity target is upstream `purejs.ts`, based on the file comments; if the target is upstream package default behavior in Node, section E item 1 is blocking.
